mathml_representations_list: <mstyle id="custom-group-4" class="custom-group"><mi id="mi_130">d</mi><mo stretchy="false">(</mo><mo>∂</mo><mi mathvariant="normal" id="mi_131">Ω</mi></mstyle>

concepts_list.name: displacement_field
concepts_list.description: The vector field representing the fluid displacement in space and time.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'u' appears multiple times in boldface, consistently denoting a vector field in the momentum and continuity equations, such as in ∂u/∂t and u⋅∇u. It is central to the governing equations and is used in both the weak form and physical equations.

concepts_list.name: test_function
concepts_list.description: A test function used in the weak formulation to enforce continuity and boundary conditions.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'w' appears repeatedly in boldface within integrals, such as in ∫Ω ρ(u⋅∇u)⋅w dΩ, and is described as a test function in a Sobolev space. It vanishes at Dirichlet boundaries and is used to derive the variational form.

concepts_list.name: domain
concepts_list.description: The main computational domain in which the fluid dynamics equations are defined.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'Ω' is used to denote the fluid domain in multiple equations, including the domain of integration and boundary conditions. It is explicitly defined as a subset of R^d and bounded by ∂Ω.

concepts_list.name: boundary
concepts_list.description: The boundary of the computational domain, where boundary conditions are applied.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol '∂Ω' appears in the boundary integral term and is referenced in the context of Dirichlet and Neumann boundary conditions. It is described as a (d−1)-dimensional surface in R^d.

concepts_list.name: density
concepts_list.description: The fluid density, a scalar field that appears in both momentum and continuity equations.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'ρ' is used in multiple equations involving mass and momentum conservation, such as in ρ(∂u/∂t + u⋅∇u) and in the continuity equation. It is a scalar field dependent on the fluid phase.

concepts_list.name: body_force
concepts_list.description: External body forces acting on the fluid, such as gravity or electromagnetic forces.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'b' appears in boldface in the momentum equation as ρb, representing a vector field of external forces acting on the fluid.

concepts_list.name: pressure
concepts_list.description: The scalar pressure field within the fluid, used in the weak form to couple with the velocity field.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'p' appears in the weak form as a term involving ∫Ω p∇⋅w dΩ, and is part of the stress tensor in the constitutive equation. It is a scalar field associated with fluid pressure.

concepts_list.name: viscosity
concepts_list.description: The scalar coefficient representing the fluid's resistance to shear deformation.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'μ' appears in the constitutive equation as μ(∇u + ∇u^T), indicating its role in the viscous stress tensor. It is a material property of the fluid.

concepts_list.name: traction_vector
concepts_list.description: The surface traction vector at the interface or boundary, calculated as n⋅σ.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'T' appears in boldface in the boundary integral and is defined as T = n⋅σ, where n is the normal vector. It represents the force per unit area on the boundary.

concepts_list.name: time
concepts_list.description: The time variable used in time-dependent equations, such as in ∂u/∂t.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 't' appears in the time derivative ∂u/∂t and in initial and boundary conditions. It is a scalar variable that evolves over time in the physical domain.

concepts_list.name: differential_volume_element
concepts_list.description: The differential volume element used in volume integrals over the domain Ω.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'dΩ' appears multiple times in integrals, such as ∫Ω ρ(∂u/∂t + u⋅∇u)⋅w dΩ, and is used to denote the volume element in the domain. It is a standard notation in finite element methods.

concepts_list.name: differential_surface_element
concepts_list.description: The differential surface element used in surface integrals over the boundary ∂Ω.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'd(∂Ω)' appears in the boundary integral term ∫∂Ω T⋅w d(∂Ω), indicating the surface element on the boundary. It is used to integrate over the boundary of the domain.

displacement_field.description:
The vector field representing the fluid displacement in space and time.
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol 'u' appears multiple times in boldface, consistently denoting a vector field in the momentum and continuity equations, such as in ∂u/∂t and u⋅∇u. It is central to the governing equations and is used in both the weak form and physical equations.
